🌿✨ If anyone is considering a Taj redemption, do consider Taj Alibaug. I personally find it one of the most relaxing weekend getaways around Mumbai. 🏡💚

I first stayed here in Feb’21, when it was operated as Radisson, and visited again last month (Apr’26) after it became a Taj property.

The property is massive, surrounded by lush greenery 🌳, peaceful landscapes, and even mango trees loaded with fruits 🥭. The vibe is calm, serene, and perfect for slowing down from city life.

🚢 Pairing the staycation with the M2M Ferry makes the trip even better. The drive from the ferry terminal to Alibaug is beautiful, with greenery on both sides of the road (though the last ~4 km leading to the property is in poor condition for last 5 years at least😅).

But once you enter the resort… it feels like a different world ✨

You can spend your time enjoying:
🏊 Large swimming pool
🚴 Cycling around the property
⛳ Mini golf course
🎮 Indoor/outdoor games
🍽️ Multiple restaurants including multi-cuisine & specialty dining
🌿 Long peaceful walks amid nature
😌 Simply doing nothing and relaxing

During both our stays, we spent 2 nights, and I genuinely feel this property deserves at least a 2-night stay to fully experience and enjoy everything it offers.

If you have Taj vouchers, NeuCoins, Epicure benefits, or are planning a Taj redemption, this property is worth considering for a perfect weekend escape from Mumbai ❤️

items.title:
🚨 Considering the increasing frauds during deliveries, Swiggy Instamart has recently implemented a dual-OTP verification system for delivery of 🪙 Gold Coins — and I found it quite reassuring 👍

Here’s how it works:

1️⃣ You receive the first OTP when you place the order.

2️⃣ When the delivery partner arrives, you share the first OTP. Once entered into the system, you receive second-level OTPs — 1 OTP for each gold coin ordered.

👉 Ordered 3 gold coins? You’ll receive 3 separate OTPs. Keep them with you for now.

3️⃣ The delivery partner then opens the package in front of you and shows each coin individually 🧐

4️⃣ If everything looks correct and you’re satisfied ✅, share the OTPs one by one, and against each OTP, the corresponding gold coin is handed over.

⚠️ If you find anything suspicious or incorrect, do NOT share the second-level OTP(s) and reject the delivery.

💰 You should receive a full refund in such cases.

I found this concept very useful and satisfying. 👏

📦 Not sure if other e-commerce platforms have also implemented it already. If not, then they should also consider implementing similar safeguards for Open Box Deliveries to improve customer trust and reduce fraud.

items.title:
🏝️ Spending Time in Resorts vs Sightseeing: My simple thumb-rule while travelling ✈️🏨👇👇👇

Whenever I visit beach destinations 🌊☀️ (Like South East Asia, Mauritius, Seychelles, Maldives etc.), I prefer staying at luxury beachfront resorts 🏖️✨

And if I’ve chosen a luxury property, I make sure to spend at least one full day doing absolutely nothing 😌🍹
✔️ Relax by the pool 🏊
✔️ Explore resort facilities 🌴
✔️ Enjoy private beaches 🌅
✔️ Have long breakfasts & slow evenings 🍽️

Because if you’re paying for a premium resort, you should actually experience it, not just sleep there 😄

👉Sometimes I visit a destination only to spend time at beautiful resort 🏝️✨

✅✅✅ Not every trip needs a packed itinerary or sightseeing checklist. Some places are worth visiting simply to unwind, enjoy the property, and slow down 💆‍♂️🌅

👉👉👉On the other hand, when visiting Europe, Singapore, Dubai or sightseeing-heavy destinations 🏙️🚆, where most of the day is spent outside exploring, I’m perfectly happy staying at value-focused hotels like ibis, ibis Styles, Novotel, Courtyard, Fairfield, AC Hotels, or good standalone properties 🏨💰

Reason is simple:

👉 If you’ll be out exploring for 12+ hours daily, why spend heavily on ultra-luxury hotels you barely use? 🤷‍♂️

For me, hotel choice depends on travel style:
🏝️ Resort vacation → Spend more on stay
🏙️ Exploration trip → Save on hotel, spend on experiences
✨ Pure relaxation trip → Choose destination based on the resort itself

This simple approach has helped me enjoy luxury where it matters while keeping overall travel costs (or points outgo ☺️) under control 💯✈️

What’s your travel style — Luxury resort person or explorer who only needs a bed? 😄👇

RT by @DoBaniye: 🚨Periodic Review by Amex : Pay attention☝️

I got this screenshot from a friend who's account was on hold for receiving payments from multiple 3rd party sources 

➡️What it means for you ?

Normally, any credit card in India is issued for personal use and outstanding payments are to be made from the card holders account only . 

Banks cause periodic audit of the card holders based on their risk based algorithm that flags such sensitive accounts 

If you cannot substantiate the received payments , consider your account to go ahead for closure for violation of terms and all the MR points might get forfeited too . 

✅What to do ?

Ensure you pay the dues from your own savings account only and try to avoid taking payments from 3rd party . 

At times , receiving money from blood relatives and family seems possible and acceptable in normal course . In other scenarios, its against the MITC of the card issuing bank 

🔁 RT and share with your close circles to remain cautious

items.title:
“Why are people still using Millennia or SimplyClick when Infinia exists?” 🤔

That’s like asking why people buy Hyundai or Maruti when Mercedes exists 🚗😄

The reality is — less than 1% people can even qualify for such super-premium cards:

💳 Infinia → Requires ~₹5L monthly in-hand salary  
💳 DCB Metal → Needs ₹2.5L monthly in-hand salary  
💳 HSBC Premier → ₹50L TRV or ~₹3L monthly salary  
💳 Magnus for Burgundy → ₹30L TRV or ~₹3L monthly in-hand salary and hefty 35k annual fee 

✅✅✅ Yes, there are many useless cards that bank salespeople push aggressively  
❌But comparisons should be fair and contextual. How many of SimplyClick users can be recommended Infinia 🤔

👉Lately, I’ve seen many posts mocking users of entry-level cashback cards and calling them “foolish” for not choosing premium airmiles cards ✈️

Some even mock SBI Cashback Card users because they prefer simple cashback instead of converting reward points into business-class flights.

I personally use cards like Infinia, EPM and Atlas for almost all my spends, and I primarily redeem points for luxury travel 🌍✈️  
For me, airmiles are extremely valuable because I would have spent cash on premium travel anyway.

👉People often forget one important principle of finance:

💰 “Cash is King.”

For many people, guaranteed 5% real cashback is FAR more valuable than a limited-use 15% return in airmiles.

Because cashback:
✅ is simple & effortless 
✅ is liquid  
✅ can be used anywhere  
✅ has zero redemption complexity  

Whereas airmiles are valuable mainly for people who actively travel and know how to optimise them.

I once received a Montblanc pen worth ₹40,000 as a prize 🖊️  
But for me, it had very little practical value because I’m not into luxury pens. Honestly, I wouldn’t have spent even ₹5,000 from my own pocket to buy it.

That’s exactly how rewards work too.

👉 Value is personal.

A great card for one person can be completely useless for another.

Unfortunately, FOMO pushes many people into premium travel cards without understanding:
• reward optimisation  
• airline programs  
• hotel loyalty programs  
• award availability  
• transfer strategies  
• redemption calculations  

As a result, many end up paying huge annual fees without extracting meaningful value 😅

Airmiles & travel cards are AMAZING for people who:
✈️ love luxury travel  
🌍 take foreign trips at least once a year  
🏨 enjoy premium hotel stays  
📈 can spend ₹5–6L+ annually on cards to earn meaningful airmiles  
🧠 enjoy learning optimisation strategies  

But they may NOT make sense for people who:
❌ Very occasionally travel by flight  
❌ prefer saving money over luxury experiences  
❌ don’t want complexity  
❌ don’t have enough annual spends to generate meaningful rewards  
❌ don’t want to spend time learning airmiles ecosystems  

Personal Finance is personal.  
The “best” credit card depends entirely on:
👉 your income  
👉 spending pattern  
👉 lifestyle  
👉 travel goals  
👉 willingness to optimise rewards  

Not everyone needs a super-premium airmiles card.  
And not everyone should be made to feel inferior for preferring simple cashback cards 🙌
